title: Buildprint Mapper OS
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: Agent-session workflow for mapping an existing repo into a source-independent phase-driven Buildprint packet with comprehensive AI-readable phase objectives instead of tiny slice/gate schema fragments.
plainDescription: Use Mapper OS when you have a working repo and want an agent to turn it into a reusable Buildprint: map the source, classify the artifact, choose scope, then emit a phase-driven execution manual with BUILDPRINT.md, 00-questions.md, 01-project-setup.md, 02-uiux-decision.md, 03-phases, and HANDOVER.md.
promise: A repo-to-Buildprint workflow that preserves scope fidelity and emits a readable phase-driven packet that makes agents build real product loops instead of schema-shaped slop.
whatYouGet: A selected-buildprint/ v3 executable packet: BUILDPRINT.md, 00-questions.md, 01-project-setup.md, 02-uiux-decision.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/, HANDOVER.md
Comprehensive phase files with How to implement this phase, Building objective, DO NOT, Minimum proof before moving on, and Handoff note
Setup foundation and UI/operator decision before phase work
whatYouNeed: GitHub/private repo access if the source is private
A human scope choice when multiple candidates exist
Permission to use any private source code
includes: A selected-buildprint/ v3 executable packet: BUILDPRINT.md, 00-questions.md, 01-project-setup.md, 02-uiux-decision.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/, HANDOVER.md
Comprehensive phase files with How to implement this phase, Building objective, DO NOT, Minimum proof before moving on, and Handoff note
Setup foundation and UI/operator decision before phase work
risks: Packet overfits source internals instead of observable behavior
Generated phases become generic dashboard work
Agent writes proof-looking prose instead of building a usable loop
Provider/deployment/security boundaries are hidden instead of named
Non-product artifacts are forced into product-app UI phases
checks: Selected output uses v3 phase-driven packet files
No slices/, gates/, templates/teams/, templates/runner/, 02-architecture.md, 03-ux-contract.md, 04-handover.md, 04-review.md, or 05-handover.md in selected packet
Every phase has required comprehensive headings and a substantial Building objective
Phase flow rejects placeholders, functionless buttons, mocks-as-proof, and fake success
HANDOVER.md names built, verified, blocked, not proven, and next actions

trustBadges.label: Phase-driven execution
trustBadges.detail: Selected packets use comprehensive phase objectives instead of tiny schema fragments.

trustBadges.label: Anti-fake-success
trustBadges.detail: The packet requires explicit bans on placeholders, dead controls, mocks-as-proof, fake provider success, and overclaiming.

trustBadges.label: Setup before phases
trustBadges.detail: Project architecture, proof strategy, env contract, and UI/operator identity are established before phase implementation.

trustBadges.label: Scope-preserving mapper
trustBadges.detail: Mapper OS separates source facts from guesses and asks for a human scope choice when multiple artifacts are plausible.

howToUse.title: 1. Point it at a repo
howToUse.detail: Open the source repo in your coding agent, or give the agent a repo URL/local path. If the target is unclear, Mapper OS asks which repo URL or local path to map.

howToUse.title: 2. Bootstrap Mapper OS
howToUse.detail: Run agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json and follow .buildprint/next-agent.md. Do not use an agb map command.

howToUse.title: 3. Discover, then choose scope
howToUse.detail: The agent inspects the repo and proposes candidates. For broad repos, it stops and asks which candidate to extract.

howToUse.title: 4. Emit the v3 packet
howToUse.detail: The selected packet contains BUILDPRINT.md, 00-questions.md, 01-project-setup.md, 02-uiux-decision.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/, and HANDOVER.md.

howToUse.title: 5. Build phase by phase
howToUse.detail: A downstream builder reads the active phase only, builds the smallest real product path, verifies it, repairs fake-success shortcuts, and records handoff facts.

resultChecklist: Selected output uses v3 phase-driven packet files
No slices/, gates/, templates/teams/, templates/runner/, 02-architecture.md, 03-ux-contract.md, 04-handover.md, 04-review.md, or 05-handover.md in selected packet
Every phase has required comprehensive headings and a substantial Building objective
Phase flow rejects placeholders, functionless buttons, mocks-as-proof, and fake success
HANDOVER.md names built, verified, blocked, not proven, and next actions
copyPrompt: Use the Buildprint Mapper OS Buildprint to map a target repo in the current agent session. Bootstrap exact snapshots with agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md. Emit a v3 phase-driven packet, not slices or gates.
